sections.title: Starting off
Talking to Ur-tag or Ambassador Alivjar

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: Headman Ur-tag, I cannot stress enough how important it is that this train link be completed on time. The mining companies have taken out considerable loans...

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: I understand your concerns, Ambassador, but you must realise that the discoveries must change the whole schedule. To remove them too hurriedly would be a crime against knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: It's the new underground train link being built between our city and the dwarven city of Keldagrim.

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: Shh! What if they're a human spy come to steal our engineering secrets?

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: It will be the pinnacle of dwarven engineering! A great leap forward from the old steam-powered minecarts! A monument to the power and ingenuity of the Consortium!

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: And it would be finished by now, except that they found some old goblin bones they want to dig up.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: We've unearthed some very important artefacts that were buried during the Dorgeshuun Civil War. They could shed important light on our history.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: [player name], do you have any experience with archaeology?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I passed my archaeology exam at the Digsite!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Excellent! We have not had a major archaeological project for generations, so if you could give us some help we would appreciate it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd love to help!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Splendid! The path to the train tunnel is in the south- west of the city.

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: Perhaps now we can get this finished quickly and get on with laying the tracks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do your own digging!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Oh well. We will continue at our own pace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do in the city?

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Why don't you visit the market? I hear there is quite a craze for surface foods at the moment, so you might be able to sell something there.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: You might also want to visit the caves to the south of the city. There are many monsters to fight there, and Zanik apparently found ways to cross over their heads!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you decide to open the city?

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: For trade, and for fostering goodwill between races! I am sure that it will benefit both our races.

sections.title: Talking to Ambassador Alvijar
Talking to Ur-tag after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: How is the archaeological dig going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not finished yet.

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: I wish you could work more quickly. The new train link is very important to the dwarven economy.

sections.title: Talking to Ambassador Alvijar after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: I hope you're hurrying up with that archaeological dig. Every moment we wait wastes more of the Consortium's money!

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: Headman Ur-tag, I cannot stress enough how important it is that this train link be completed on time. The mining companies have taken out considerable loans...

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: I understand your concerns, Ambassador, but you must realise that the discoveries must change the whole schedule. To remove them too hurriedly would be a crime against knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: It's the new underground train link being built between our city and the dwarven city of Keldagrim.

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: It will be the pinnacle of dwarven engineering! A great leap forward from the old steam-powered minecarts! A monument to the power and ingenuity of the Consortium!

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: And it would be finished by now, except that they found some old goblin bones they want to dig up.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: We've unearthed some very important artefacts that were buried during the Dorgeshuun Civil War. They could shed important light on our history.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: [player name], do you have any experience with archaeology?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I passed my archaeology exam at the Digsite!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Excellent! We have not had a major archaeological project for generations, so if you could give us some help we would appreciate it.

sections.title: The archaeological dig
Talking to Zanik

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Hi [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Zanik. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm having a look at the things that are being dug up here!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It's really amazing to think that these things were used by my ancestors hundreds of years ago. Tegdak thinks that some of them may even have been brought down here from the surface by the early Dorgeshuun!

sections.title: The builders

lines.speaker: Builder
lines.text: I hope those goblins hurry up with their digging. It's so frustrating having to stop work when the train link is nearly finished!

lines.speaker: Builder
lines.text: Bah! These goblins don't appreciate how important this train link is! It'll be the greatest dwarven achievement of this generation, and it's being held up just so they can pick at old broken things in the ground.

lines.speaker: Builder
lines.text: I hope the Consortium will understand it's not our fault the work will be finished late. This is the most important job of my career!

lines.speaker: Builder
lines.text: I'm looking forward to riding on this train once it's finished. It'll be so much faster than the old steam-powered minecarts. It'll be the fastest machine in Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Builder
lines.text: Will you tell those goblins to get on with their digging? We've got a job to finish here.

sections.title: Talking to Tegdak
Talking to Tegdak without any artefacts and not delivering any before

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: How are you getting on with extracting the artefacts?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't got any yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Also, I lost my trowel.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Well, you'll have to free up some inventory space before I can give you a new one.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Never mind. Have another one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I lost my specimen brush.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Well you'll have to free up some inventory space before I can give you a new one.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Never mind. Have another one.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Oh, hello surface-dweller. I'm afraid we're a bit busy cleaning specimens at the moment so I can't help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ur-tag asked me to help out with the dig. I have an archaeology qualification!

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Oh! In that case we could use your help.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: There are some ancient goblin artefacts buried in the ground around here. We want to extract them, but we don't have much experience and could end up breaking them.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Could you extract them, clean them, and then give them to me please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Here, take this trowel and specimen brush.

sections.title: Digging up an artefact
Talking to Tegdak with an unclean artefact

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Thanks for extracting that artefact, but you'll need to clean it as well.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: I've got a sample brush here but you'll have to make space in your inventory for me to give it to you.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Here, use this brush to clean the artefact.

sections.title: Attempting to clean an artefact without a brush
Cleaning an artefact on the specimen table
Dropping an artefact
Using an item that isn't an artefact on Tegdak

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: What? No, extract the artefacts please.

sections.title: Talking to Tegdak with an artefact
Talking to Tegdak without any artefacts and delivering at least one

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: How are you getting on with extracting the artefacts?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't got any more yet.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: It looks like a fragment of armour.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Look at the design! That's a lot like the design of the goblin armour I saw on the surface.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: That confirms that our ancestors retained a lot of customs from the surface even after they came down here. Our guards didn't start wearing lighter chainmail until after the Civil War.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: That's all the artefacts we needed to extract. Thanks again, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Now, the most interesting one is this ancient mace. It's the only intact weapon, and I'm pretty sure it dates back to the time when the Dorgeshuun were on the surface.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: It's covered in symbols, but I can't decipher them. Zanik, could you take it to the scribe? He might know what they mean. I'll finish up the dig here.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Sure thing! Come on, [player name], let's go talk to the scribe

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have another one.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: It looks like a piece of a shield.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Look at that stress fracture. It looks like this shield broke pretty easily.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: I would guess that this shield was made during the Dorgeshuun Civil War and not handed down from the time on the surface.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: It seems that even by the time of the Civil War, our ancestors were beginning to lose their skills at making weapons.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: It looks like a piece of a helmet.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: A helmet? So those are eye-holes? They look too small.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: This was from the early days of Dorgesh-Kaan. It must have been before our ancestors' eyes reached their current size.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: I'm not sure. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It looks a bit like a sword, doesn't it? I saw some people using them on the surface.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: The early Dorgeshuun must have used a much larger variety of weapons than we do now.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: I'm not sure. It's got some kind of blade... Is it a spear tip?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh, I know what it is! It's an axe head!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Axes are these things they use on the surface for cutting down trees. I think they can be used as weapons too.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Our ancestors must have brought it down from the surface, then. There would be no need for them to make axes when they were down here, as there are no trees.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: It's amazing to think that we're handling objects that date back to the time of the war of the gods!

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: It's... Actually, what is it? It's a sort of stick with a lump on the end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it's a mace.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: A mace?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A kind of weapon.

sections.title: Talking to Tegdak after handing in all six artefacts

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Thanks for your help. There's not much left for us to do here except pack everything up. Have you shown that mace to the scribe yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

sections.title: The goblin scribe
Talking to Zanik
Picking up Zanik from the railway

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Shall we go and talk to the scribe about this ancient mace now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Let's go then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh well. Let me know when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the mace?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I've got it! I'll hold onto it until we've shown it to the scribe. Shall we go?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I've got a strange feeling about this mace, [player name]. Maybe when we find out about it, it will shed some light onto my destiny. Come on, let's show it to the scribe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something else for a bit.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: All right. I'll go back and help finish the dig.

sections.title: Talking to Ambassador Alvijar

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: Thank you for helping with the dig! Ur-tag tells me it will be finished soon, and then we will be able to resume work!

sections.title: Talking to Ur-tag

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Greetings, surface-dweller. Welcome to Dorgesh-Kaan.

sections.title: Talking to Oldak with Zanik

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Ah, Zanik. Would you like to see the results of my experiment with nature runes?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Not right now, Oldak. I'm helping [player name]!

sections.title: Talking to the goblin scribe with Zanik
Talking to the goblin scribe again

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Hello, Zanik. Have you found out about that mace yet?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Not yet. Come on, [player name]! We should go and ask the goblin generals about it!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, hello Zanik, hello [player name]. How is the dig going?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: [player name] helped us to finish the dig! Look what she dug up!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Hmm... that is interesting. What do you think it is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a mace.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: [player name] says that's a kind of weapon.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: I can see how. I certainly wouldn't want to be hit with it!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We think it's a magical weapon from the surface. Can you decipher the symbols on it?

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: No, I'm afraid not. Our written language has developed so much since then that it's totally indecipherable to me.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh well.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Wait a moment. [player name], didn't you say you talked to some surface goblins when you were trying to find your way through our mines?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I talked to the generals in the Goblin Village.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Perhaps you could talk to them again. They might know more about weapons than we do.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Good idea!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'd rather not travel all that way over the surface, though. I wonder if Oldak could use magic to help us get there?

sections.title: Talking to Zanik
Picking up Zanik from the railway

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Shall we go see Oldak now? I'm sure he'll have a way of getting us to the Goblin Village quickly!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I can't believe we're going to the Goblin Village on the surface! I'm finally going to see how my distant relatives live!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Actually, I'm a little nervous about what I'll find there...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

sections.title: Talking to Tegdak

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Thanks for your help. There's not much left for us to do here except pack everything up. Have you shown that mace to the scribe yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! Now we're going to go to the Goblin Village to ask the surface goblins about it!

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Good idea! Maybe Oldak can help you get there.

sections.title: Talking to Oldak
Talking with Zanik in Goblin Village

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: So this is how goblins on the surface live! Come on, [player name], lets talk to them!

sections.title: Leaving Goblin Village

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Where are we going?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'll meet you back in Dorgesh-Kaan, [player name].

sections.title: Getting Zanik from the railway

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Come on [player name], let's get back to the Goblin Village on the surface! Shall we go?

sections.title: Talking to Zanik in Dorgesh-Kaa

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Let's go then!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What are we doing here, [player name]? We should get to the Goblin Village!

sections.title: Returning to Oldak again

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Ah, Zanik. How did you get on at the Goblin Village?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We haven't finished there actually. Do you think you could teleport us there again?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Of course.

sections.title: Talking to goblins around the village

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Who you?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Dorgeshuun?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Yes! We're another tribe of goblins who live underground. We've been cut off from the surface for generations but now we're back.

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: What you want?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Who is in charge here?

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: That General [Wartface/Bentnoze]. He live in big hut.

lines.speaker: Goblin (opposing armour colour)
lines.text: No General [Bentnoze/Wartface] is leader!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Why are you fighting?

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: He say [Bentnoze/Wartface] is leader but real leader is [Wartface/Bentnoze]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These goblins fight all the time, Zanik. Come on, Wartface and Bentnoze are both in the big hut.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Ah, Zanik. Would you like to see the results of my experiment with nature runes?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Not right now, Oldak. We wanted to see if you could make Moving-Over-Distance Spheres to somewhere other than the city.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Oh, yes. My research along those lines has been coming along very well. I think I could make a Moving-Over-Distance Sphere to anywhere you like, given a little work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're trying to take this ancient goblin mace to the Goblin Village on the surface. Do you think you could help us to get there?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Hmm. The Goblin Village, you say? Yes, I think I could do that. Just a moment.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: There you go. I've made you each one to get you to the Goblin Village and one to bring you back here.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Thanks Oldak! Okay, [player name], let's go!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Ow, my eyes...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: So this is the Goblin Village! It's so different from Dorgesh-Kaan, isn't it, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: [player name], where are you?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aaaah!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Sorry about that, [player name]! Oldak's calculations aren't always exact!

sections.title: The goblin generals
Walking into the centre of the village

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Who you?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Lost tribe?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: That's right! We've been cut off from the outside world for thousands of years but now we're back!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: So legend true!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: What you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell us anything about this mace?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: That holy mace of Big High War God.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: In time of great war, strongest goblin warriors carry maces like this.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: They use them to take favour of gods away from enemy. If another god protecting enemy, goblin warrior use this mace and favour of god is stolen away!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oh. A holy mace of the War God.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: You not look very happy.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: You should praise Big High War God for giving you holy mace!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Dorgeshuun don't have quite the same attitude to the gods as you on the surface do.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: All goblins worship Big High War God! Without him we nothing!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: You not real goblin! You filthy human!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I am a goblin!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: You not even look like goblin! You have big eyes and wrong colour skin!

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: She is goblin!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Shut up Grubfoot!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: No let him speak.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: She is goblin. And she more than that...

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Last night I have dream. I see this new goblin come into village, holding up holy mace.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: All goblins gather around her. Not just from village, from all over world. She is Chosen Commander!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Chosen Commander?

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: That story from goblin holy book. We not had Commander of all goblins since time of great war.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Holy book say someday Big High War God send Chosen Commander to lead us to victory over whole world.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: That what I see in dream. It her destiny.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: My destiny...

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: That not true! She cannot be Chosen Commander! She not proper goblin! She not worship Big High War God!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: But what if she is? If we ignore her, Big High War God strike us down!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: All right. I not believe you Chosen Commander. But we let you talk to people in village.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Grubfoot! Tell people in village to gather around! Dorgesh goblin will speak to us all!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Goblins listen up! This Zanik, she from Dorgeshuun tribe!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: She say she Chosen Commander!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: No, I-

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: We not know what she have to say. But in case she Chosen Commander, we let her speak. Speak, Zanik!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Help, [player name]! What should I say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Goblins... goblins of the Goblin Village. I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun. You may know the legend of the Dorgeshuun, but let me tell you our own version.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Thousands of years ago, the Big High War God forced all goblins to fight in a terrible war.

lines.speaker: Goblins
lines.text: Big High War God! Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: No! Listen! The god commanded the Dorgeshuun to fight a battle they could not win!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: You weak! You hide instead of fight!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You don't need to think in those terms! We've lived for centuries without war, and without gods, and-

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Aaaargh!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Unk!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: We under attack!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Scatter!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: The shots came from the top of that tower, [player name]! Can you see anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are two snipers up there.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I can't get anywhere without coming into their line of fire, but I think you could get around the side of the buildings to the ladder.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

sections.title: Upon defeating the two H.A.M. members
Talking to the generals

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I rescue Zanik?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Pink robe man say they digging tunnel under Lumbridge Swamp Caves. You go there to rescue Chosen Commander.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Sergeants wait for you at entrance to Lumbridge Swamp Caves.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: You take ancient goblin mace. Maybe it be useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the mace.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: We know! Some goblin find it, give it back here.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Me tell you human not clever enough! He lose mace.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: You want mace, you give us 1,000 coins. Maybe you not lose it again if you have to pay!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. Here you go.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: You stupid human! You not have enough money!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: When come back, bring 1,000 coins.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: You take ancient goblin mace now and rescue Chosen Commander!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Remember, mace has power to rob enemy of power of Prayer!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Pink robe man say they digging tunnel under Lumbridge Swamp Caves. You go there to rescue Chosen Commander.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Sergeants wait for you at entrance to Lumbridge Swamp Caves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Well then, human no get mace.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: But me not think you able to kill Sigmund with no mace.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: So if you care about big-eyed friend, you find 1,000 coins!

sections.title: Talking to Ambassador Alvijar

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: Thank you for helping with the dig! I hear that the work on the tunnel is now complete and the first train will be making the journey very soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've got to stop the train! Sigmund is planning something terrible down there!

lines.speaker: Ambassador Alvijar
lines.text: What are you babbling about? Everyone was cleared out of the tunnel when we completed work. There's no reason to stop the train.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the rail station via Dorgesh-Kaan

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sigmund!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: You can never defeat me! I've been training my Combat by killing goblins around Lumbridge!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You... you murderer!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Quick, [player name]! Shoot him while I have him distracted!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Aaaaah! My eyes!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Don't even think about it, [player name]! Make one move and I'll slit her throat!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Your little goblin friend has a role in my plan! I'm taking her to the tunnel we've built from the Lumbridge Swamp Caves to the new dwarven train track.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: When the cave goblins find that the dwarven train has killed their hero, there will be war between them! Saradomin's chosen children can watch as two vile underground races wipe each another out!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: A-ha-ha-ha-ha-haaa!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Are they gone?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: What happen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sigmund has kidnapped Zanik!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: He kidnap Chosen Commander? You must rescue her!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Human will need help. You take our best warrior, Sergeant Mossfists.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: No! Sergeant Slimetoes best warrior! Take him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We don't have time for this! Why don't I take both warriors?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Grubfoot! Fetch Mossfists and Slimetoes and tell them to meet [player name] in Lumbridge Swamp!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: You take ancient goblin mace. Maybe it be useful.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: You rescue Chosen Commander and she lead us all to glory!

sections.title: Talking to the Sergeants in the Lumbridge Swamp
Talking to the Sergeants in the Lumbridge Swamp Caves

lines.speaker: Sergeant Mossfists/Slimetoes
lines.text: Look, there new hole with ladder just next to climby-out rope. We go down there.

sections.title: Talking to the Sergeants on the surface after talking to them in the cave

lines.speaker: Sergeant Mossfists/Slimetoes
lines.text: What we waiting for? We must go down into tunnel and rescue Chosen Commander!

lines.speaker: Sergeant Slimetoes
lines.text: You [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Mossfists
lines.text: Generals say we help you rescue Chosen Commander.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Slimetoes
lines.text: It too important for puny human to do on own. You just get killed.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Mossfists
lines.text: So we come to look after you so you not die.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Slimetoes
lines.text: Grubfoot say pink robe man take Chosen Commander down this hole into new tunnel.

sections.title: The under-underground
Coordinating with the sergeants
With both sergeants

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Both of you follow me.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Slimetoes
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One of you wait here.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Slimetoes
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Both of you wait here.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Slimetoes
lines.text: Okay.

sections.title: With one sergeant

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait here.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Slimetoes
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Follow me.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Slimetoes
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh no! Goblins!

lines.speaker: Sergeant Mossfists
lines.text: Ha ha! Puny human scared of me!

lines.speaker: Sergeant Slimetoes
lines.text: No he scared of me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's probably just gone to get reinforcements. He'll be back in a few moments with more guards.

sections.title: The distraction
Guards engaging in combat with the sergeants

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Goblin!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Kill it!

sections.title: Sergeants when in combat with the guards

lines.speaker: Sergeant Mossfists/Slimetoes
lines.text: Quick! Me keep human busy!

lines.speaker: Sergeant Mossfists/Slimetoes
lines.text: Ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Sergeant Mossfists/Slimetoes
lines.text: Take that!

sections.title: If the guards spot the player

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You there!

sections.title: If the guard at the end spots the player

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Intruder!

sections.title: Talking to either of the sergeants while they are fighting the guards

lines.speaker: Sergeant Mossfists/Slimetoes
lines.text: You go quick! I keep guard busy!

sections.title: Climbing down to the railway
If Sigmund is attacked with melee

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Saradomin protect me from melee!

sections.title: If Sigmund is attacked with Ranged

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Saradomin protect me from missiles!

sections.title: If Sigmund is attacked with Magic

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Saradomin protect me from magic

sections.title: Attempting to unite Zanik during the fight

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Got you!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: At last I have defeated you, you filthy goblin!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Any moment now the first dwarf train will come thundering down this tunnel and crush you! And then the goblins and the dwarves will go to war!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Ha ha ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You monster!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: No, YOU'RE the monster! That's why you and all your filthy species must die!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: We meet again, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: This time your goblin friend cannot help you. You are on your own and have no way to overcome my prayers!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Help! Help!

sections.title: Attacking him with the ancient mace special

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: What's happening? That weapon is overcoming Saradomin's protection! You've taken away the last of my prayer energy!

sections.title: Defeating Sigmund

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Bah! You win again, [player name]! But someday, somehow, I will have my revenge!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Quick, [player name]! Untie me before the train comes!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: It gives me great pleasure to declare this new train link open!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: [player name], Zanik has told me about your exploits. It seems that all the Dorgeshuun owe you our lives once again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All in a day's work for an adventurer like me!

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: As a reward, I suggest you keep the ancient goblin mace that you helped to excavate.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Most of the Dorgeshuun would be happy if such a war-like artefact was kept outside the city, and I am sure you will keep it in safe hands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Ur-tag!
